response.justification: The candidate lacks direct C++ experience in industrial automation or high-performance systems, with most roles centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang; while they have brief C++ exposure from 2014–2015 at National Instruments, there is no evidence of recent, sustained C++ development, STL usage, multi-threading, or IEC 62443 compliance — all core requirements for this role.
strengths: Strong full-stack and cross-platform development experience across web, embedded, and IoT systems.
Demonstrated leadership in open-source projects and technical mentoring through workshops and CI/CD infrastructure.
Proven ability to deliver scalable, event-driven systems with real-world deployments (e.g., GISCollective, AI-agent tools).
concerns: No recent C++ work in the last 9+ years; core technical requirement not met.
Lacks exposure to industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) and IEC 62443 security standards.
No evidence of message brokers, MQTT, or cloud-native industrial platform development.
